zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France於1338年2月3日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內文斯時，她的父親Duc Pierre De Bourbon I26歲，母親Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon24歲。她於1350年4月8日與Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France在法國o-阿爾培斯o萊昂結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和9個女兒的父母。她去世於1378年2月6日在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年40歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯沙吉登尼斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France于1338年2月3日出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內文斯时，她的父亲Duc Pierre De Bourbon I26岁，母亲Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon24岁。她于1350年4月8日与Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France在法国o-阿爾培斯o萊昂结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和9个女儿的父母。她去世于1378年2月6日在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年40岁，埋葬在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯沙吉登尼斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourbon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from a village in Allier, the site of the (now ruined) castle of Bourbon, or from another place called (Le) Bourbon, mainly in the southern part of France. The placename is derived from a Celtic and pre-Celtic element borb- denoting a hot spring.

History: The progenitor of a large part of the American bearers of the surname Bourbon was Jean-Pierre Bourbon from Salins-les-Bains in Jura, France, who settled in MO in the middle of the 19th century. However, many bearers of the surname claim a connection with the house of Bourbon, which provided generations of monarchs of France and Spain and other European royalty. It takes its name from the castle of Bourbon in Allier, which was held by Adhémar, a 9th-century noble. His descendant Beatrice, heiress of Bourbon, married Robert of Clermont, 6th son of King Louis IX of France, in 1272, and these two are considered founders of the royal house. Robert's son Louis was created Duke of Bourbon in 1327. By the 16th century they had added much of southern France, the dukedom of Vendôme, and the kingdom of Navarre to their fiefs, and in 1589 a Bourbon succeeded to the throne of France as Henry IV. His grandson, Louis XIV, called ‘the Sun King’, reigned 1643–1715, presiding over a golden age of French literature and art and attempting to establish French supremacy in Europe. The Bourbons ruled France until the Revolution in 1793 and again 1815–48. The present claimants to the French throne are descended from Louis XIV's brother, Philippe, Duke of Orléans. The Bourbons acquired the Spanish throne in 1700, when a grandson of Louis XIV succeeded as Philip V; they ruled until 1931 and were restored, in the person of King Juan Carlos, in 1975. The Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies (i.e. Sicily and Naples), 1759–1861, and Bourbon dukes of Parma, 1748–1860, were descended from the Spanish branch of the family.

variants.name: Borbon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Jesus, Marta, Miguel Angel, Tomas, Ademar, Alfredo, Ana Maria, Bernabe, Berta, Carlos.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France was born on 3 February 1338, in Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, her father, Duc Pierre De Bourbon I, was 26 and her mother, Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon, was 24. She married Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France on 8 April 1350, in Lyon, Rhône, Rhône-Alpes, France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 9 daughters. She died on 6 February 1378, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 40, and was buried in Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France est née le 3 février 1338, à Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Duc Pierre De Bourbon I, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France le 8 avril 1350, à Lyon, Rhône, Rhône-Alpes, France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 9 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 février 1378, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France am 3. Februar 1338 in Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Duc Pierre De Bourbon I 26 und ihre Mutter Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon 24. Sie hat am 8. April 1350 in Lyon, Rhône, Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 9 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. Februar 1378 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France è nata il il 3 febbraio 1338 nel Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Duc Pierre De Bourbon I, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France il 8 aprile 1350, nel Lyon, Rhône, Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 9 figlie. Morì il 6 febbraio 1378, nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolta a Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de Franceが1338年2月3日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスヴァル-デ-マルンヴィンセンスで生まれた時，父Duc Pierre De Bourbon Iは26歳，母Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbonは24歳であった。1350年4月8日にフランスルhoネ-アルペスルhoンリオンでCharles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de Franceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼女は1378年2月6日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで40歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスセアイネ-サイント-デニスサイント-デニスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France(이)가 1338년 2월 3일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-데-마니 빈스네스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Duc Pierre De Bourbon I(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1350년 4월 8일 프랑스 ho니-알프스 ho니 리에서 Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1378년 2월 6일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 세이인-세인트-데니스 세인트-데니스에 묻혔다.

pt.03 February 1338–06 February 1378: 3 de fevereiro de 1338–6 de fevereiro de 1378
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France nasceu em 3 de fevereiro de 1338, em Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Duc Pierre De Bourbon I, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France em 8 de abril de 1350, em Lyon, Rhône, Rhône-Alpes, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 9 filhas. Ela faleceu em 6 de fevereiro de 1378, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, com 40 anos, e foi sepultada em Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France родилась 3 февраля 1338 г. в местности винценнесь, вал-де-марн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Duc Pierre De Bourbon I было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France 8 апреля 1350 г. в местности лен, рoн, рoне-алпесь, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 9 дочерей. Она умерла 6 февраля 1378 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 40 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт-денис, сине-сеинт-денис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.03 February 1338–06 February 1378: 3 de febrero de 1338–6 de febrero de 1378
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France nació el 3 de febrero de 1338, en Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Duc Pierre De Bourbon I, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Isabelle de Valois d'Orleans, Duchesse de Bourbon, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France el 8 de abril de 1350, en Lyon, Rhône, Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 6 de febrero de 1378, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultada en Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia.
